verbGlossary.ADDRESSES: {source} directly responds to or manages a specific {target} through formal regulatory or compliance action (e.g., permit application addresses regulatory risk)
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZES: {source} response grants formal approval for {target} mitigation action to proceed (e.g., RFI response authorizes a mitigation action for a design ambiguity)
verbGlossary.CLOSES OUT: {source}document formally resolves and retires a residual {target} upon project completion (e.g., punch list closes out residual risk when all deficiencies are corrected)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} imposes material or performance requirements that limit exposure to {target} (e.g., specification constrains supply chain risk by narrowing acceptable products)
verbGlossary.CONSTITUTES: {source} event itself represents or embodies {target} trigger in the project record (e.g., notice of delay constitutes a risk trigger for schedule claims)
verbGlossary.CONTROLS: {source}document establishes procedures that actively manage and reduce {target} metric (e.g., safety plan controls incident rate through mandated protocols)
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} management metric trends in parallel with {target} metric over time (e.g., daily log completion rate correlates with incident rate)
verbGlossary.DIRECTS: {source} orders specific field action to address {target} condition (e.g., site instruction directs a mitigation action for an unsafe condition)
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTES: {source} formally circulates {target}-related information to responsible parties (e.g., transmittal distributes updated risk register to the project team)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED IN: {source} or discussion is formally captured within project correspondence for the record (e.g., {target} documented in project correspondence)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS: {source}records the occurrence or evidence of {target} condition (e.g., daily report documents a safety risk observed on site)
verbGlossary.ENACTS: {source}document formally puts {target} response strategy into contractual effect (e.g., change directive enacts a risk response by ordering immediate work)
verbGlossary.ESCALATES: {source} raises the severity or visibility of {target} to higher authority or stakeholders (e.g., correspondence escalates an unresolved risk to senior management)
verbGlossary.EVIDENCES: {source}record provides proof that {target} trigger or risk condition exists (e.g., photo evidences construction execution risk such as a structural defect)
verbGlossary.EXECUTES: {source}document item carries out a specific step within {target} mitigation plan (e.g., action plan line item executes a mitigation action assignment)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES: {source} reveals or uncovers a previously unidentified or underestimated {target} (e.g., RFI exposes design risk when field conditions conflict with drawings)
verbGlossary.FORMALIZES: {source} converts an informal {target} condition into a formal contractual notice (e.g., notice of delay formalizes schedule risk into a documented claim basis)
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source}document sets the standards against which {target} is managed (e.g., safety plan governs safety risk through mandated procedures)
verbGlossary.IMPLEMENTS: {source} document puts a risk mitigation strategy into operational practice (e.g., action plan implements mitigation actions defined in {target} register)
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source} formally starts {target} identification or response process (e.g., RFI initiates a risk trigger when a design conflict is raised)
verbGlossary.INVOKES: {source} document activates a contractual {target} allocation clause (e.g., notice of delay invokes risk allocation provisions for time extensions)
verbGlossary.MITIGATES: {source} or document action reduces the probability or impact of {target} (e.g., submittal mitigates supply chain risk by confirming material compliance early)
verbGlossary.PREDICTS: {source} volume or frequency metric serves as a leading indicator of an emerging {target} (e.g., RFI volume density predicts design risk escalation)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source} assigns a measurable value to the potential financial impact of {target} (e.g., pricing request quantifies financial risk of a proposed scope change)
verbGlossary.REDUCES: {source} activity lowers the severity or residual exposure of {target} (e.g., O&M manual reduces construction execution risk by ensuring proper operational handoff)
verbGlossary.REFERENCED BY: {source} or document set is cited within {target} register entry to identify the affected scope (e.g., drawing set referenced by risk register for location-specific risks)
verbGlossary.REPORTED AT: {source} metric is formally presented and reviewed during a documented meeting (e.g., {target} reported at progress meeting per meeting minutes)
verbGlossary.RESOLVES: {source} response eliminates or sufficiently addresses {target} condition (e.g., RFI response resolves design risk by providing definitive design direction)
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} session formally evaluates {target}s for status and decision-making (e.g., meeting reviews open risk register items)
verbGlossary.REVISES: {source} modifies {target} landscape by changing scope, requirements, or contract terms (e.g., addendum revises risk register when new scope items introduce new risks)
verbGlossary.SATISFIES: {source}document fulfills a contractual {target} transfer requirement (e.g., certificate of insurance satisfies an insurance requirement)
verbGlossary.SCOPES: {source} or document defines the physical or functional boundary used to assess {target} impact (e.g., drawing sheet scopes the area evaluated in an impact assessment)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} metric or event provides an early warning that {target} is emerging or worsening (e.g., submittal rejection rate signals supply chain risk issues)
verbGlossary.STANDARDIZES: {source}establishes a consistent structure for recording or tracking {target} information (e.g., proforma form standardizes risk register entry format)
verbGlossary.SURFACES: {source}document reveals {target} trigger condition through routine project reporting (e.g., daily report surfaces a risk trigger when an unforeseen condition is noted)
verbGlossary.TRACKS: {source} records ongoing status updates for {target}s or mitigation progress (e.g., meeting minutes track mitigation action completion status)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS: {source}document shifts residual {target} responsibility to the next party or phase (e.g., warranty documentation transfers residual risk to the manufacturer or warrantor)
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS VIA: {source}document serves as the formal vehicle for shifting {target} to another party (e.g., certificate of insurance transfers risk via the insurance requirement to the insurer)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERED BY: {source} is issued as a direct result of {target} event materializing (e.g., drawing revision triggered by design risk when errors are discovered)
verbGlossary.VALIDATES AGAINST: {source} confirms compliance with requirements, reducing uncertainty about {target} (e.g., product data submittal validates material against supply chain risk specifications)
